description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/sdk:3.1 Docker image with two stages: test and publish. Add a test job named branch::test in the test stage that runs only on non‑tag commits (except tags) and executes `dotnet test -v minimal -c Release /p:CollectCoverage=true`. Add a publish job named branch::publish in the publish stage that runs only on tag commits (only tags) and executes the following commands in order: `echo ---------------------------------------------- TESTING`, `dotnet test -v minimal -c Release /p:CollectCoverage=true`, `echo ---------------------------------------------- VERSIONING`, `echo VERSION := $CI_COMMIT_TAG`, `sed -i -- "s/1\.0\.0\.0/${CI_COMMIT_TAG}/g" ./FunctionalLink/FunctionalLink.csproj`, `echo ---------------------------------------------- BUILDING`, `cd FunctionalLink`, `dotnet pack -c Release`, `cd ..`, `echo ---------------------------------------------- PUBLISHING`, `cd ./FunctionalLink/bin/Release`, `NUGET_PKG=$(ls *.nupkg)`, `echo PACKAGE := $NUGET_PKG`, `dotnet nuget push $NUGET_PKG -k $NUGET_KEY -s https://api.nuget.org/v3/index.json`, `cd ../../..`. The publish job pushes the built NuGet package to nuget.org.
